'''Simon Baldwin''' (born 31 March 1975) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s who played as a {{rlp|pr}}, or {{rlp|sr}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

Baldwin started his career at [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] before joining [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax Blue Sox]] in October 1994. He then spent a season with [[Sheffield Eagles]] in 1999. After a second spell at Leigh, he joined [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in 2003. He left the club in 2006, and went on to play for [[Rochdale Hornets]] and [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]].

While at Halifax, he also represented [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] under-21s,  and [[England national rugby league team|England]] in 1995.
